text.title: Production Trace Mining Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every trace-mining spec must enforce.

rule.statement: The methodology fires on a named trigger (event, threshold, schedule). Vague triggers ('when needed') rejected.
rule.rationale: Methodologies without explicit triggers fire constantly or never; a named trigger makes cadence enforceable.

rule.statement: Output shape is the trace-mining-spec.json schema. Free-form output rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Free-form output is one-off and dies on completion; shaped output is reusable.

rule.statement: Every label inference cites the row id(s) and inference rule that justified it. Unevidenced labels demoted to hypotheses.
rule.rationale: Unevidenced labels become opinion; citations make the spec reviewable.

rule.statement: Spec names a single accountable owner. Outputs without named owner tagged stale at next cadence.
rule.rationale: Anonymous outputs do not produce change.

rule.statement: Spec reviewed against outcomes at a published cadence (monthly or quarterly). No measurable change after two reviews → deprecate.
rule.rationale: Methodologies without outcome review become folklore.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → run the 5-step procedure and emit trace-mining-spec.json.
PII-scrub recipe missing OR fewer than 3 instances per year OR no named owner → skip this methodology.
